rows.description: Dec: 28 to Francis King his Bill
rows.notes: Clear, well-formed handwriting for description and numbers. Pounds/shillings/pence columns aligned by ruling; pence has a clear halfpenny loop interpreted as 0.5.
rows.english_description: Payment to Francis King for his bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Francis King for his bill, amounting to £1 9s 6d.

rows.description: to ye Dean of Ch. Ch. sent for being Venison
rows.notes: Amount columns clear (2s 6d). Description legible though abbreviation 'Ch. Ch.' (Christ Church) is used; chosen reading based on typical ledger phrasing.
rows.english_description: Payment to the Dean of Christ Church for venison.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Dean of Christ Church for venison, amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.notes: Short entry; numeric columns perfectly aligned and read as 0 pounds, 1 shilling, 0 pence. Text clear.
rows.english_description: Payment for corn price.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for corn price, amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.notes: Clear description and amounts in columns (1 pound). Vertical ruling used to align columns; values within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Payment for lighting lamps.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for lighting lamps, amounting to £1 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Description and numbers mostly unambiguous; pound/shilling/pence alignment clear. Minor uncertainty in surname spelling 'Munday' but amount reading is clear.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Munday for his bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Munday for his bill, amounting to £8 13s 6d.

rows.description: 17 to Mr Weston four years Interest Due at xmas 1743
rows.notes: Large pound figure (128) is clearly written and aligned in the pounds column. Text mentioning 'four years Interest' and 'xmas 1743' is legible.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Weston for four years' interest due at Christmas 1743.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Weston for four years' interest due at Christmas 1743, amounting to £128 0s 0d.

rows.description: to Mr Weston upon bond by common consent
rows.notes: Amount 32 pounds clearly in pounds column; description legible and consistent with preceding Weston entry.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Weston upon bond by mutual agreement.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Weston upon bond by mutual agreement, amounting to £32 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Simple entry identical format to previous 'Corn price' rows; numeric columns clear.
rows.english_description: Payment for corn price.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for corn price, amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.description: to Mr Blop one Rham of Imp. Paper
rows.notes: Amounts (2-12-6) are clear and aligned. Surname and the word before 'one Rham' are ambiguous in the handwriting ('Blop'/'Blox'/'Bloxk'). Chose 'Blop'/'Rham' as closest visible reading; spelling uncertain.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Blop for one ream of imperial paper.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Blop for one ream of imperial paper, amounting to £2 12s 6d.

rows.notes: Numeric columns (16-5-2) read clearly. Surname appears as 'Teasler' (could be 'Teasle' or 'Teasler'); chosen reading based on visible looped letters.
rows.english_description: Payment to Teasler for his bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Teasler for his bill, amounting to £16 5s 2d.

rows.notes: Amounts (13-17-0) are well aligned. Description likely 'J Cook for Gardin(s)' or 'Gardens'—the final word is abbreviated/blurred; recorded exactly as seen 'Gardis'.
rows.english_description: Payment to J Cook for gardens.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to J Cook for gardens, amounting to £13 17s 0d.

rows.notes: Short entry with clear amounts 0-8-6. Name 'Mrs Heritage' is legible.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mrs Heritage.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mrs Heritage, amounting to £0 8s 6d.

rows.notes: Amounts (41-8-4) are clear and aligned. Surname read as 'Cozens' (could be 'Coezens' but 'Cozens' is the best match to the script).
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Cozens for his bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Cozens for his bill, amounting to £41 8s 4d.

rows.description: May 19 to wine to entertain Mr Wilmot in C.Pk.
rows.notes: Amount (0-1-6) clear. Description has minor abbreviation 'C.Pk.' and slightly cramped script; context (wine to entertain Mr Wilmot) is clear.
rows.english_description: Payment for wine to entertain Mr Wilmot in Christ Church Paddock.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for wine to entertain Mr Wilmot in Christ Church Paddock, amounting to £0 1s 6d.

rows.description: June 12 to Mr Boylis Works for y Library
rows.notes: Amounts (5-5-0) read clearly. Surname appears as 'Boylis' / 'Baylis'—handwriting uncertain; 'Works for y Library' is legible and typical phrasing.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Boylis for works in the library.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Boylis for works in the library, amounting to £5 5s 0d.

rows.description: July 7 to Mrs Combs her Bill
rows.notes: Amounts (2-4-0) clear. Name 'Mrs Combs' and phrase 'her Bill' are legible.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mrs Combs for her bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mrs Combs for her bill, amounting to £2 4s 0d.

rows.description: 10 to Mr Will. Talbot his Plate money
rows.notes: Amount (5-0-0) clear. Abbreviated given name 'Will.' and 'Talbot' readable; context 'Plate money' is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr William Talbot for his plate money.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr William Talbot for his plate money, amounting to £5 0s 0d.

rows.description: Aug 4 to Mr Townsend his Bill
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings/pence aligned (44-5-11). Pence shows an additional small flourish interpreted as three farthings (0.75); the flourish is slightly cramped causing minor uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Townsend for his bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Townsend for his bill, amounting to £44 5s 11d.

rows.description: 10 to Landtax for ye year 1742. & 1743. ye Aid
rows.notes: Numeric columns (14-8-0) clear. Description contains old-style abbreviations 'ye' and 'Aid' but is legible.
rows.english_description: Payment for land tax for the years 1742 and 1743.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for land tax for the years 1742 and 1743, amounting to £14 8s 0d.

rows.description: 20 brought to acct wch was pd Newman for Wine to entertain Coll. Ten.
rows.notes: Amount 0-3-0 is clear. Several abbreviated words ('acct', 'wch', 'pd') and names ('Newman', 'Coll. Ten.') are slightly cramped but interpretable in context.
rows.english_description: Amount contributed to account paid to Newman for wine to entertain the college tenants.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Amount contributed to account paid to Newman for wine to entertain the college tenants, amounting to £0 3s 0d.

rows.notes: Same short format as earlier 'Corn price' rows; numbers and text very clear.
rows.english_description: Payment for corn price.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for corn price, amounting to £0 1s 0d.

rows.description: 25 to Salisbury Carrier for 2 Boxes from Mr Crabb
rows.notes: Amounts (0-9-4) clear. Description legible; 'Salisbury Carrier' and 'Mr Crabb' are readable.
rows.english_description: Payment to the Salisbury carrier for two boxes from Mr Crabb.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Salisbury carrier for two boxes from Mr Crabb, amounting to £0 9s 4d.

rows.description: Oct. 10 to Mr Horner for Paper Ink &c.
rows.notes: Amounts aligned (0-17-4) and readable. Description clear; '&c.' abbreviation visible.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Horner for paper, ink, and other supplies.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Horner for paper, ink, and other supplies, amounting to £0 17s 4d.

rows.description: to Gedley for binding two Buttry Books
rows.notes: Amount (1-4-0) clear. Surname 'Gedley' (or 'Gidley'/similar) uncertain in handwriting; 'Buttry Books' (buttery) is legible but archaic spelling.
rows.english_description: Payment to Gedley for binding two buttery books.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Gedley for binding two buttery books, amounting to £1 4s 0d.

rows.notes: Short description and amount (0-15-0) very clear; month/day placement 'Oct. 5' is readable.
rows.english_description: Payment for wine.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for wine, amounting to £0 15s 0d.

rows.description: 20 to Bayliffe for carrying away Rubbish
rows.notes: Amount (2-3-0) clear. 'Bayliffe' and purpose 'carrying away Rubbish' are legible.
rows.english_description: Payment to the bailiff for carrying away rubbish.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the bailiff for carrying away rubbish, amounting to £2 3s 0d.

rows.notes: Numeric-only total line (386-12-6 1/4). Pounds/shillings/pence alignment clear; pence shows a small fractional mark interpreted as one farthing (0.25). No accompanying text on same line.
Numeric-only subtotal (179-16-2) well aligned and legible; no fraction present.
Numeric subtotal (90-7-5 1/4). Pence has a small quarter-farthing/quarter mark read as 0.25; slight ink faintness but alignment supports reading.
Numeric subtotal (94-5-5 1/4) with quarter mark on pence similar to prior line. Alignment consistent; fraction small but visible.
Boxed/boxed-sum line showing total (751-1-8 3/4). The text label is abbreviated and the fraction (three farthings) reading is slightly uncertain due to cramped script inside the box.

Handwritten Latin/abbreviated phrase 'Re Soluti quæerunt' (spelling uncertain) followed by the same total (751-1-8 3/4). Lettering is flourished and partly faded; numeric reading consistent with boxed total above.
Phrase 'Deductis receptis quæerunt' (old Latin phrasing) is present; numeric (739-2-9) is fairly clear though ink is slightly faded. Chosen reading based on alignment and visible digits.

Final balance line reads as 'rest debet Mno Comput.' with amount (11-18-3 3/4). Shillings 18 is valid; pence shows three farthings (0.75). Handwriting cramped and slightly smudged, causing moderate uncertainty.
Signature-line at bottom with a name and title (Rector). Cursive signature is difficult to read; 'Dogcumbe' is a best-effort transcription and may be incorrect.
Second signature line; likely 'Franciscus Webber' with 'subRector' or similar title. Very cursive and compressed, high uncertainty in exact spelling.
Signature with what appears to be 'Jacobus Copland' and an abbreviation 'Th. B.' (degree). Cursive letters unclear; transcription is approximate.
Another signature, read as 'Car: Webber A.M.' (possibly 'Car.' = Carolus/Charles). Handwritten abbreviation and initials are ambiguous.
Cursive signature poorly legible; letters read as 'Wm. Keyl?? M.' but this is highly uncertain. Included because it is a visible signature line with no amounts.
Final cluster of small signatures; this line is faint and cursive. Transcription 'Gul. Pye?' is speculative based on letter shapes.
Last visible signature line; heavily abbreviated and connected script. 'Tho. Bray' is a best guess; very low confidence due to fading and flourish.
